resources.string: Change the app language without changing your phone language.
Weekly and monthly breakdowns, consistency score, risk analysis, and goal prediction.
Shows how stable your daily step pattern is over time
A combined score based on consistency and goal completion
Your activity is %1$s by %2$d%% compared to last week.
Your activity peaks during the %1$s. Your routine looks consistent.
Goal completed today. Your streak is safe.
Recent activity dropped by about %1$d%%. There is a high risk of losing your streak.
Recent activity is down by about %1$d%%. Stay active to protect your streak.
You are close. Keep moving to maintain your streak.
You are on track. Reaching your goal looks very likely.
You are slightly behind, but reaching your goal is still very possible.
You are behind schedule. A short walk now would help.
You are at risk. Consider a focused walking session.
Rescue charges protect your streak when activity drops. You choose when to use them.
Your streak is close to ending
Great rhythm — keep your daily momentum going.
You are still in a safe zone. A short walk helps.
Activity has dipped. Move today to stay protected.
Your streak is at risk. Walk soon or use a rescue charge.
Rescue protection is active. Stay active to rebuild momentum.
Restore your streak before the recovery window ends.
Use 1 Rescue Charge to protect your streak and restore it for 24 hours.
Restore your streak before the recovery window expires.\nYour milestones, stats and progress will remain intact.
Recover your current streak instantly and continue progressing without losing your milestones.
Receive streak protection and milestone notifications.
Smart notifications for streak risk, protection milestones and goal proximity.
View your walking workouts and session details.
Select a day to inspect its sessions.
Tap a session to inspect its details below.
A compact read of your recent workout pattern.
This session shows sustained effort with strong pace and duration.
A balanced session with healthy rhythm and good continuity.
A short burst session that works well for quick movement breaks.
A steady session. Extending duration slightly could improve its training value.
No recent workout sessions yet. As sessions build up, this section will surface your weekly movement pattern.
Your weekly profile shows strong consistency, good volume, and sustained movement quality.
You are maintaining a balanced weekly pattern. Slightly longer sessions could improve overall output.
You are building a routine. More frequent sessions would make your weekly pattern stronger and more stable.
Your current workload is still light. Short but regular walks can quickly improve this trend.
Your weekly movement rhythm at a glance.
A focused summary of the selected day.
Low activity today. A short walk can protect your streak.
%1$d more steps unlock the next shield hour.
Finish today’s goal to secure bonus protection.
Your shield is almost empty (%1$dh %2$dm left). Rescue is available.
You are quieter than usual. A short walk now helps keep your rhythm stable.
Health Connect is not available on this device

Deep sleep, REM, light split, and awake moments need sensors on your wrist (smartwatch or ring) or sleep data synced from Health Connect. Phone-only or manual logs estimate total sleep only — Deep, REM, and Awake stay at 0%% until a wearable or Health Connect provides real stage data.

Heart rate is captured when your wearable shares it with Health Connect or the app has sensor access during sleep. Without that link you will see \"No data\" — your sleep duration and score still work.
Use Quick log to add a sleep record, or connect Health Connect to sync automatically.
This will remove synced sleep data from the last 7 days.
This sleep record will be permanently removed.
This day has %1$d sleep records (main sleep, naps, or synced sessions). Remove all of them for %2$d %3$s?
Detailed sleep quality trends and summary insights are available here.
A clean overview, history, tracking, deeper analysis, and richer reporting live here.
This section provides additional metrics and long-term summaries to analyze your sleep patterns in greater depth.
Select a day to manage your sleep record
Edit sleep record for this day
Add the time and how it felt.
This session is too short to save.
This session looks too long. Please check the time range.
This looks more like main sleep than a nap.
Keep the reminder active every day.
Keep the notification quiet while the alarm sound remains controlled separately.
Vibrate while the alarm is ringing.
Choose a system ringtone or a local audio file.
%1$s. Reminder set for %2$s. Tap to open StepForge and manage the alarm.
%1$s. Alarm is ringing for %2$s. Tap to open alarm.
A clean bedtime reminder built around your theme.
A clean bedtime reminder. Exact access can make timing sharper.
If this access is off, reminders can be delayed or skipped while the app is closed.
If this access is off, the alarm can ring in the background but the stop screen may not open automatically.
Control your alarm with your voice while it is ringing.
StepForge uses the microphone only while your alarm is ringing, so you can stop or snooze the alarm hands-free. Audio is not recorded or stored.
Voice commands are disabled. You can still stop or snooze the alarm with the buttons.
StepForge listens only while the alarm is ringing. It does not record or store audio. You can say “Stop” to turn off the alarm or “Snooze” to delay it by 5 minutes.
No data recorded for this day
Track your sleep to unlock insights
Your bedtime has been very consistent recently. This regularity boosts sleep quality.
Going to bed at %1$s disrupts your circadian rhythm. Aim for 10 PM – midnight.
You slept %1$dh %2$dm. Adults need 7–9 hours for full recovery.
You went to bed within an optimal time window.
Your REM ratio is %1$d%% — ideal is 20–25%%.
Deep sleep ratio is %1$d%%. Regular exercise helps.
Your sleep score is %1$d/100. Keep up this routine!
Your sleep heart rate was %1$d bpm — excellent cardiovascular recovery.
Add a short detail for later.
A lighter sleep stage that helps the body settle and transition between deeper phases.
The most restorative stage. Your body repairs tissue, restores energy, and supports immunity.
Important for memory, learning, and emotional balance. Most vivid dreams happen here.
Short wake periods can happen naturally during the night and are not always a problem.
Great work. You are beating your weekly rhythm.
Good start. Keep your rhythm going.
A short session can still count.
Estimated from pace, duration, and calories.
Start moving to build your day.
Your workout history will appear here.
Open your full workout history and session details.
Zones show how your workout time was split by effort. Higher zones mean harder movement, lower zones mean easier warm-up or steady activity.
Tip: Try to keep a balanced mix instead of staying in one zone every day.
%1$d workout days generated your %2$s km total this week.
